samples.xml_description:
<module id="calendar">
    <description>Implement a perpetual calendar with seconds, minutes, and hours.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal.</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">Current hours.</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">Current minutes.</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">Current seconds.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAX_SECONDS">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_MINUTES">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_HOURS">23</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        This module implements a perpetual calendar by continuously updating the seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal. 
        The seconds value increments every clock cycle, and when it reaches 59, it resets to 0 and increments the minutes value. 
        When both the minutes and seconds values reach 59, the hours value increments by 1, and the hours value wraps around to 0 when it reaches 23, allowing the module to accurately represent the current time at all times.
    </implementation>
</module>
